content:
RORK — FULL VERCEL AUDIT AND SAFE REMOVAL

OBJECTIVE

Find every place where IVX still depends on Vercel and remove only the obsolete Vercel code, configuration, routes, environment variables, deployment hooks and domains.

Do not delete working production APIs until the replacement is identified and verified.

Current target architecture:

- Expo React Native / Expo Router for mobile
- Owner-controlled GitHub repository
- Supabase for auth, database, storage and realtime
- Render or the verified production backend for APIs
- No Vercel dependency in the production Android APK
- No temporary Vercel preview URLs
- No duplicate web/backend architecture

PHASE 1 — IDENTIFY THE ACTIVE CODEBASE

Return:

- active GitHub repository
- active branch
- current HEAD SHA
- Expo app root
- backend root
- whether a separate Vercel frontend exists
- whether a separate Vercel API exists
- current production domains
- current production API URL

Do not assume all Vercel code should be removed.

First classify each Vercel dependency as:

- ACTIVE AND REQUIRED
- DUPLICATE
- OBSOLETE
- PREVIEW ONLY
- DEAD CODE
- UNKNOWN — REQUIRES VERIFICATION

PHASE 2 — SEARCH THE COMPLETE REPOSITORY

Search every file for:

- vercel
- vercel.json
- .vercel
- api/
- pages/api
- app/api
- next.config
- next.config.js
- next.config.mjs
- next.config.ts
- @vercel
- VERCEL_URL
- VERCEL_ENV
- VERCEL_PROJECT_ID
- VERCEL_ORG_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_
- process.env.VERCEL
- process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC
- vercel.app
- *.vercel.app
- preview deployment URLs
- serverless
- edge function
- edge runtime
- NextRequest
- NextResponse
- Next.js
- rewrites
- redirects
- proxy rules
- deployment hooks
- Vercel analytics
- Vercel Speed Insights
- Vercel Blob
- Vercel KV
- Vercel Postgres
- Vercel Cron
- Vercel functions

Also search for all hardcoded domains and API base URLs.

Return every match with:

- file
- line number
- code
- purpose
- whether mobile uses it
- whether web uses it
- whether backend uses it
- removal decision
- replacement target

PHASE 3 — AUDIT THESE FILES FIRST

Audit:

- package.json
- package-lock.json
- yarn.lock
- bun.lock
- bun.lockb
- pnpm-lock.yaml
- app.json
- app.config.js
- app.config.ts
- eas.json
- vercel.json
- next.config.*
- expo-env.d.ts
- .env
- .env.local
- .env.production
- .env.example
- src/config/*
- lib/config/*
- lib/api.*
- lib/supabase.*
- services/*
- api/*
- pages/api/*
- app/api/*
- server/*
- backend/*
- scripts/*
- GitHub Actions workflows
- deployment scripts
- README deployment instructions

PHASE 4 — FIND THE REAL API ROUTES

Create a full API inventory.

For every route return:

- route path
- HTTP method
- file
- current host
- consumers
- production traffic status
- replacement route
- migration status

Examples:

- /api/health
- /api/auth/*
- /api/members
- /api/investors
- /api/projects
- /api/deals
- /api/reels
- /api/uploads
- /api/media
- /api/chat
- /api/admin/*
- /api/analytics

Do not delete any Vercel API route until:

1. Its replacement exists.
2. Expo points to the replacement.
3. The replacement returns the expected status.
4. Production data matches.
5. Authentication works.
6. Uploads work.
7. QA passes.

PHASE 5 — AUDIT EXPO MOBILE DEPENDENCIES

Search the Expo app for:

- EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL
- API_URL
- BASE_URL
- BACKEND_URL
- WEB_URL
- VERCEL_URL
- fetch(
- axios.create
- ky.create
- GraphQL client URLs
- WebSocket URLs
- upload URLs
- auth callback URLs
- deep-link callback URLs

For every request show:

- screen or module
- endpoint
- current host
- expected host
- authentication method
- timeout
- error handling

The production APK must not call:

- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- 10.0.2.2
- 169.254.*
- 192.168.*
- loca.lt
- ngrok
- temporary Vercel preview URL
- old vercel.app domain

Replace obsolete endpoints with the verified live API host.

PHASE 6 — SAFE REMOVAL PLAN

Remove only after verification:

- vercel.json
- .vercel directory
- Vercel deployment scripts
- Vercel-specific GitHub workflows
- obsolete Vercel environment variables
- @vercel packages
- Next.js packages if no active Next.js app remains
- Vercel Analytics
- Vercel Speed Insights
- Vercel preview domains
- dead API routes
- duplicate serverless functions
- obsolete redirects and rewrites
- old documentation
- temporary preview references

Do not remove:

- active public website code
- required API logic
- shared business logic
- authentication logic
- database migrations
- upload logic
- media processing logic

Move shared logic before deleting old Vercel files.

PHASE 7 — PACKAGE CLEANUP

Audit package.json for:

- next
- react-dom
- @vercel/*
- vercel
- serverless packages
- web-only packages
- duplicate React versions
- duplicate Supabase clients

Remove unused dependencies.

Then run:

- clean install
- dependency audit
- TypeScript check
- lint
- Expo Doctor
- Android bundle
- backend tests

Return the dependency diff.

PHASE 8 — ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE CLEANUP

Create a variable inventory:

- variable name
- current platform
- current consumer
- required or obsolete
- replacement
- build-time or runtime
- present or missing

Audit:

- VERCEL_URL
- VERCEL_ENV
- VERCEL_PROJECT_ID
- VERCEL_ORG_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_*
- EXPO_PUBLIC_*
- SUPABASE_*
- RENDER_*
- API_URL
- BACKEND_URL

Do not expose secret values.

Remove obsolete Vercel variables from:

- repository examples
- build configuration
- EAS environment
- Render environment
- source code
- GitHub Actions
- documentation

PHASE 9 — DOMAIN AND CALLBACK AUDIT

Audit all domains and callbacks:

- ivxholding.com
- chat.ivxholding.com
- api.ivxholding.com
- Vercel domains
- Render domains
- Supabase auth redirect URLs
- Expo deep-link scheme
- password recovery URLs
- email confirmation URLs
- OAuth callback URLs
- CORS allowlists

Confirm which service owns each domain.

Remove old Vercel domains only after:

- DNS is verified
- SSL is active
- callbacks use the new domain
- CORS allows the new domain
- mobile auth works
- password recovery works

PHASE 10 — VERIFY NO VERCEL RUNTIME DEPENDENCY

After cleanup, run a repository-wide search again.

Required production result:

- no Vercel URL in Expo bundle
- no obsolete Vercel environment variables
- no active calls to vercel.app
- no Vercel deployment hook required
- no Vercel preview dependency
- no Vercel serverless function used by the APK

Allowed exception:

A separate public website may remain on Vercel only if explicitly approved and documented.

If any Vercel item remains, return:

- exact reason
- owner
- consumer
- removal risk
- replacement timeline

PHASE 11 — QA AFTER REMOVAL

Verify:

1. Expo Go opens.
2. APK opens.
3. Owner Login opens.
4. Manual login works.
5. Members load.
6. Investors load.
7. Projects load.
8. Deals load.
9. Reels load.
10. Video upload works.
11. Images load.
12. Chat works.
13. Admin works.
14. Logout works.
15. Password recovery works.
16. Offline launch does not black-screen.
17. API health returns 200.
18. No request goes to vercel.app.
19. No preview URL appears in logs.
20. No CORS error was introduced.

PHASE 12 — REQUIRED PROOF

Return:

- active repository
- branch
- previous SHA
- new SHA
- all Vercel matches before cleanup
- all files removed
- all files modified
- all API replacements
- environment variables removed
- dependencies removed
- final repository search results
- Expo Doctor output
- TypeScript output
- lint output
- Android build result
- APK
- APK SHA-256
- real-device video
- network log proving no vercel.app requests
- /health response
- production deployment ID

DEFINITION OF DONE

Vercel removal is complete only when:

- the Expo app and APK do not depend on Vercel
- all required routes have verified replacements
- no production mobile request uses vercel.app
- no obsolete Vercel configuration remains
- Expo Go and APK pass QA
- authentication, data, reels, uploads and admin still work
- GitHub SHA and APK build SHA match

Do not delete first and test later.

Inventory → replace → switch traffic → verify → remove → verify again.
